title: Finite Interval Arithmetic Shadow Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite interval-arithmetic bad product upper-bound QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact rational interval replay computes product_upper = 100020001/100000000 while a malformed first-order shortcut claims product_upper <= 5001/5000, isolating a compact scalar bound conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional interval-arithmetic rows only when they add distinct dependency, outward-rounding, mixed-sign, nonlinear-expression, QF_FP, bit-vector, or numerical-honesty pressure; keep general interval-analysis soundness and floating-point interval packages in future theorem or QF_FP lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed rational input intervals, exact sum interval, exact positive product interval, width arithmetic, and product-upper excess as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route the false product upper-bound claim through checked QF_LRA/Farkas evidence after replay computes the exact product endpoint.
Expose the pack through rational-interval, exact-vs-floating, real-analysis, and numerical-analysis learner and consumer query surfaces.

Keep arbitrary expression interval analysis, dependency handling, outward rounding, hardware floating-point endpoints, and stability guarantees marked as future Lean, QF_FP, bit-vector, or numerical-honesty work until formal routes exist.
